title: Who’s responsible for bug reports on old software versions?
excerpt: Consider this hypothetical: An operating system (OS) ships version 1.5 of a piece of software. Meanwhile, the latest version of that software is 3.0. A user on that OS experiences an issue in versi…
content_html:
Consider this hypothetical:

An operating system (OS) ships version 1.5 of a piece of software.

Meanwhile, the latest version of that software is 3.0.

A user on that OS experiences an issue in version 1.5, or has an idea for a new feature. Who should they contact?

This hypothetical becomes concrete due to the existence of discrete-release OSs, like Ubuntu, Debian, openSUSE Leap, and Linux Mint. These intentionally freeze on certain versions of the software they ship for a certain period of time — even if newer versions have already been released upstream of them.

It’s in the news right now because of a recent kerfuffle over in Linux Mint specifically; a developer of GNOME Calendar asked Linux Mint to patch out support links and change the branding, and later followed up with a fairly inflammatory blog post after the issue was locked for understandable reasons.

This saddens me because the miscommunication was preventable, and now a good portion of the discussion surrounding the topic is about tone rather than the topic itself — a predictable outcome of not caring about tone. But I digress.

I think it’s an important topic, so I thought I’d share my take on the situation.

Are software developers responsible?

Software devs wrote the software. The software broke. End of story.

If only life were that simple!

Having been on the receiving end of thousands of un-actionable bug reports about old versions of KDE’s software in discrete-release OSs for issues that were fixed months or years ago (but not backported by the OS distributor!)… I can tell you it’s very frustrating.

But we in the free, open-source software (FOSS) world make our software available via free software licenses; we need to be prepared for OSs distributing our software in ways we didn’t anticipate or aren’t thrilled about, and bug reports from their users. That’s life.

Our solution in KDE is a bot that automatically closes bug reports for versions of Plasma that are out of support, and we may eventually broaden the system to cover our apps and frameworks, too.

It’s not perfect, but it mostly works out. I can tell because most of the new Plasma bug reports I see these days are from users of rolling-release OSs like Arch, OpenSUSE Tumbleweed, and Fedora KDE (which is not truly rolling, but it’s close enough), and I’d say most are actionable.

It kind of stinks for users of discrete-release OSs, who get a robot telling them that the work they just put in to report the bug was useless. But they also benefit from rolling-release users effectively being their free quality assurance (QA). Trade-offs.

Are distributors responsible?

If my car breaks, I blame the car company — not the vendor who sold them the part that broke.

(well, maybe I blame them too if I’m a car nerd, but that’s the exception!)

Ultimately OS distributors are the car company here, assembling the final product. It’s their job to do adequate QA and work with their vendors (upstream software devs) to make sure that final product sparkles.

Of course, a new car costs many months’ salary and includes a multi-year warranty, while most FOSS OSs don’t and are distributed for free. So expectations need to be tempered a bit.

This is where I think communication breaks down. A lot of free-of-charge FOSS OSs advertise themselves really positively, promising the sun, the moon, and the stars. Whereas the truth for many is that they’re assembled by a small team with a shoestring budget (or none at all) from years-old software offered by grumpy developers they don’t have a great relationship with.

Users then don’t understand the full picture of who’s responsible for what, or the level of support they should expect and from whom.

This is the reason why KDE Linux includes tons of qualifiers and provisos in its marketing material. We don’t want to over-promise! It’s a small project built by a small team. I think every piece of free software should be clear about who should use it… and who shouldn’t. Don’t over-promise! It’s a recipe for frustration and disappointment on all sides.

And if you want an OS with professional backing, you’ll likely need to pay for it.

So what’s the solution?

As a distributor, I think you need to sort out what kind of relationship you want to have with the developers of the upstream software you ship.

Do you want to be able to have a distant relationship? Then I recommend abandoning discrete releases and adopting the rolling release approach.

In this model, you take upstream software the moment it’s released — or at least soon afterwards. Then the conflict disappears! Almost all bugs become upstream bugs, and you can direct users to upstream devs without getting any push-back from them. Everything remaining is an issue that you can fix in your OS.

Rolling release OSs can still have good relationships with their upstreams, of course. But it isn’t as critical.

Don’t want to be a rolling release? That’s fine. Then you need to work closely with your upstreams.

Communicate your users’ complaints and bugs upstream, help drive fixes, and then ship them. Many software developers already release bug-fix versions in addition to feature versions. If they do… ship them! Don’t ignore them; this will make your upstreams grumpier.

If any of your upstreams release a “long-term support” version of their software, ship that. If they don’t, you can even work with them to create one if they’re amenable to the idea, and help to support it.

This is more work, obviously. So if your team is small, it may not be feasible to do for every piece of software you ship. But you can try for the most important ones: the Linux kernel, systemd, Mesa, Libinput, PipeWire, NetworkManager, BlueZ, udisks, CUPS, and KDE or GNOME.

But that’s the important point: shipping a high-quality discrete release OS is more work than producing a competent rolling release. No way around it.

So a way to make this approach more feasible is to reduce your OS’s scope of concern.

For example, you can delegate app distribution to a third party like Flathub, the Snap store, AppImageHub, etc. This lets you focus on just the base OS and its desktop environment, while apps are rolling, which means they’ll accept bug reports about them. Personally, I think there’s a lot to like about this model.

It also helps if you don’t let the software get too out of date. Up to 6 months old? Your upstreams are likely fine with this, and will accept bug reports. 12 months? Mostly fine. 2 years? That starts to get painful for your upstream developers if users are still reporting bugs to them. 3 year or more? Very painful. We pretty much have to direct them to their OS vendor.

What’s not the solution?

Package 2+ year-old frozen releases of as much upstream software as you can, and then ignore the upstreams, their bug-fix releases, and their complaints about this.

This is the worst of all worlds!

  • Users get outdated software that’s full of bugs and security holes fixed long ago
  • Software developers get un-actionable bug reports from angry and confused users
  • Distributors get frustrated communications from software developers that damage or destroy important relationships

Please don’t do this. It isn’t sustainable over time, and will cause a leak of the most desirable users who make an active choice to use your OS.

How do I know this?

Well, I don’t, but I can make an educated guess based on some of the few statistics we do have. A big one is the OS market share on ProtonDB as compiled by BoilingSteam, which counts Linux gamers:

In 2019, the “rolling and rolling-ish (e.g. Fedora)” OSs made up a little over 36% of the total users.

In 2026, they’re up to 71%.

This isn’t everyone, of course; only gamers using ProtonDB. But still, it’s quite a change. Clearly gamers believe rolling-release OSs cater to their needs better than discrete-release OSs do.

So, if you ship a discrete release OS, and you don’t want to or can’t switch it to a rolling or semi-rolling release cadence, please please please work with your upstreams! As a (sadly now only merely occasional) upstream KDE software dev, I can tell you my favorite distributors to work with are the ones who engage with KDE and help get problems solved. It’s a lot of fun when all parties focus on problem-solving.

blocks.html_en: Consider this hypothetical:

sentences.en: Consider this hypothetical:
sentences.zh: 考虑这个假设：

blocks.html_en: An operating system (OS) ships version 1.5 of a piece of software.

sentences.en: An operating system (OS) ships version 1.5 of a piece of software.
sentences.zh: 操作系统 (OS) 附带软件的 1.5 版本。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, the latest version of that software is 3.0.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, the latest version of that software is 3.0.
sentences.zh: 同时，该软件的最新版本是3.0。

blocks.html_en: A user on that OS experiences an issue in version 1.5, or has an idea for a new feature. Who should they contact?

sentences.en: A user on that OS experiences an issue in version 1.5, or has an idea for a new feature.
sentences.zh: 该操作系统的用户在版本 1.5 中遇到问题，或者有新功能的想法。

sentences.en: Who should they contact?
sentences.zh: 他们应该联系谁？

blocks.html_en: This hypothetical becomes concrete due to the existence of discrete-release OSs, like Ubuntu, Debian, openSUSE Leap, and Linux Mint. These intentionally freeze on certain versions of the software they ship for a certain period of time — even if newer versions have already been released upstream of them.

sentences.en: This hypothetical becomes concrete due to the existence of discrete-release OSs, like Ubuntu, Debian, openSUSE Leap, and Linux Mint.
sentences.zh: 由于 Ubuntu、Debian、openSUSE Leap 和 Linux Mint 等离散版本操作系统的存在，这一假设变得具体。

sentences.en: These intentionally freeze on certain versions of the software they ship for a certain period of time — even if newer versions have already been released upstream of them.
sentences.zh: 它们故意将其发布的软件的某些版本冻结一段时间——即使它们的上游已经发布了较新的版本。

blocks.html_en: It’s in the news right now because of a recent kerfuffle over in Linux Mint specifically; a developer of GNOME Calendar asked Linux Mint to patch out support links and change the branding, and later followed up with a fairly inflammatory blog post after the issue was locked for understandable reasons.

sentences.en: It’s in the news right now because of a recent kerfuffle over in Linux Mint specifically; a developer of GNOME Calendar asked Linux Mint to patch out support links and change the branding, and later followed up with a fairly inflammatory blog post after the issue was locked for understandable reasons.
sentences.zh: 它现在成为新闻焦点是因为最近 Linux Mint 发生了一场混乱； GNOME Calendar 的开发者要求 Linux Mint 修补支持链接并更改品牌，随后在该问题出于可以理解的原因被锁定后发表了一篇相当煽动性的博客文章。

blocks.html_en: This saddens me because the miscommunication was preventable, and now a good portion of the discussion surrounding the topic is about tone rather than the topic itself — a predictable outcome of not caring about tone. But I digress.

sentences.en: This saddens me because the miscommunication was preventable, and now a good portion of the discussion surrounding the topic is about tone rather than the topic itself — a predictable outcome of not caring about tone.
sentences.zh: 这让我感到难过，因为沟通不畅是可以避免的，现在围绕这个话题的讨论很大一部分是关于语气而不是话题本身——这是不关心语气的可预见的结果。

sentences.en: But I digress.
sentences.zh: 但我离题了。

blocks.html_en: I think it’s an important topic, so I thought I’d share my take on the situation.

sentences.en: I think it’s an important topic, so I thought I’d share my take on the situation.
sentences.zh: 我认为这是一个重要的话题，所以我想分享一下我对这种情况的看法。

blocks.html_en: Are software developers responsible?

sentences.en: Are software developers responsible?
sentences.zh: 软件开发商有责任吗？

blocks.html_en: Software devs wrote the software. The software broke. End of story.

sentences.en: Software devs wrote the software.
sentences.zh: 软件开发人员编写了软件。

sentences.en: The software broke.
sentences.zh: 软件坏了。

sentences.en: End of story.
sentences.zh: 故事结束。

blocks.html_en: Software devs wrote the software. The software broke. End of story.

sentences.en: The software broke.
sentences.zh: 软件坏了。

sentences.en: End of story.
sentences.zh: 故事结束。

blocks.html_en: If only life were that simple!

sentences.en: If only life were that simple!
sentences.zh: 生活要是这么简单就好了！

blocks.html_en: Having been on the receiving end of thousands of un-actionable bug reports about old versions of KDE’s software in discrete-release OSs for issues that were fixed months or years ago (but not backported by the OS distributor!)… I can tell you it’s very frustrating.

sentences.en: Having been on the receiving end of thousands of un-actionable bug reports about old versions of KDE’s software in discrete-release OSs for issues that were fixed months or years ago (but not backported by the OS distributor!)…
sentences.zh: 收到了数以千计的关于离散版本操作系统中 KDE 软件旧版本的无法采取行动的错误报告，这些错误报告在几个月或几年前就已修复（但操作系统发行商并未向后移植！）...

sentences.en: I can tell you it’s very frustrating.
sentences.zh: 我可以告诉你这非常令人沮丧。

blocks.html_en: But we in the free, open-source software (FOSS) world make our software available via free software licenses; we need to be prepared for OSs distributing our software in ways we didn’t anticipate or aren’t thrilled about, and bug reports from their users. That’s life.

sentences.en: But we in the free, open-source software (FOSS) world make our software available via free software licenses; we need to be prepared for OSs distributing our software in ways we didn’t anticipate or aren’t thrilled about, and bug reports from their users.
sentences.zh: 但我们在自由开源软件 (FOSS) 世界中通过自由软件许可证提供我们的软件；我们需要为操作系统以我们没有预料到或不感兴趣的方式分发我们的软件以及用户的错误报告做好准备。

sentences.en: That’s life.
sentences.zh: 这就是生活。

blocks.html_en: Our solution in KDE is a bot that automatically closes bug reports for versions of Plasma that are out of support, and we may eventually broaden the system to cover our apps and frameworks, too.

sentences.en: Our solution in KDE is a bot that automatically closes bug reports for versions of Plasma that are out of support, and we may eventually broaden the system to cover our apps and frameworks, too.
sentences.zh: 我们在 KDE 中的解决方案是一个机器人，它可以自动关闭不受支持的 Plasma 版本的错误报告，并且我们最终可能会扩展系统以涵盖我们的应用程序和框架。

blocks.html_en: It’s not perfect, but it mostly works out. I can tell because most of the new Plasma bug reports I see these days are from users of rolling-release OSs like Arch, OpenSUSE Tumbleweed, and Fedora KDE (which is not truly rolling, but it’s close enough), and I’d say most are actionable.

sentences.en: It’s not perfect, but it mostly works out.
sentences.zh: 它并不完美，但基本上可以解决问题。

sentences.en: I can tell because most of the new Plasma bug reports I see these days are from users of rolling-release OSs like Arch, OpenSUSE Tumbleweed, and Fedora KDE (which is not truly rolling, but it’s close enough), and I’d say most are actionable.
sentences.zh: 我之所以能这么说，是因为我最近看到的大多数新 Plasma 错误报告都来自 Arch、OpenSUSE Tumbleweed 和 Fedora KDE（不是真正的滚动版本，但已经足够接近）等滚动发布操作系统的用户，而且我认为大多数都是可操作的。

blocks.html_en: It kind of stinks for users of discrete-release OSs, who get a robot telling them that the work they just put in to report the bug was useless. But they also benefit from rolling-release users effectively being their free quality assurance (QA). Trade-offs.

sentences.en: It kind of stinks for users of discrete-release OSs, who get a robot telling them that the work they just put in to report the bug was useless.
sentences.zh: 对于离散版本操作系统的用户来说，这有点糟糕，他们会得到一个机器人告诉他们，他们刚刚为报告错误所做的工作是无用的。

sentences.en: But they also benefit from rolling-release users effectively being their free quality assurance (QA).
sentences.zh: 但他们也受益于滚动发布的用户，有效地成为他们的免费质量保证（QA）。

sentences.en: Trade-offs.
sentences.zh: 权衡。

blocks.html_en: Are distributors responsible?

sentences.en: Are distributors responsible?
sentences.zh: 经销商有责任吗？

blocks.html_en: If my car breaks, I blame the car company — not the vendor who sold them the part that broke.

sentences.en: If my car breaks, I blame the car company — not the vendor who sold them the part that broke.
sentences.zh: 如果我的车坏了，我会责怪汽车公司，而不是卖给他们坏零件的供应商。

blocks.html_en: If my car breaks, I blame the car company — not the vendor who sold them the part that broke.

sentences.en: If my car breaks, I blame the car company — not the vendor who sold them the part that broke.
sentences.zh: 如果我的车坏了，我会责怪汽车公司，而不是卖给他们坏零件的供应商。

blocks.html_en: (well, maybe I blame them too if I’m a car nerd, but that’s the exception!)

sentences.en: (well, maybe I blame them too if I’m a car nerd, but that’s the exception!)
sentences.zh: （好吧，如果我是一个汽车迷，也许我也会责怪他们，但这是例外！）

blocks.html_en: Ultimately OS distributors are the car company here, assembling the final product. It’s their job to do adequate QA and work with their vendors (upstream software devs) to make sure that final product sparkles.

sentences.en: Ultimately OS distributors are the car company here, assembling the final product.
sentences.zh: 最终，操作系统分销商是组装最终产品的汽车公司。

sentences.en: It’s their job to do adequate QA and work with their vendors (upstream software devs) to make sure that final product sparkles.
sentences.zh: 他们的工作是进行充分的质量检查并与供应商（上游软件开发人员）合作，以确保最终产品闪闪发光。

blocks.html_en: Of course, a new car costs many months’ salary and includes a multi-year warranty, while most FOSS OSs don’t and are distributed for free. So expectations need to be tempered a bit.

sentences.en: Of course, a new car costs many months’ salary and includes a multi-year warranty, while most FOSS OSs don’t and are distributed for free.
sentences.zh: 当然，一辆新车要花费好几个月的工资，并包括多年保修，而大多数自由和开源操作系统则没有，而且是免费分发的。

sentences.en: So expectations need to be tempered a bit.
sentences.zh: 因此，期望需要稍微缓和一些。

blocks.html_en: This is where I think communication breaks down. A lot of free-of-charge FOSS OSs advertise themselves really positively, promising the sun, the moon, and the stars. Whereas the truth for many is that they’re assembled by a small team with a shoestring budget (or none at all) from years-old software offered by grumpy developers they don’t have a great relationship with.

sentences.en: This is where I think communication breaks down.
sentences.zh: 我认为这就是沟通中断的地方。

sentences.en: A lot of free-of-charge FOSS OSs advertise themselves really positively, promising the sun, the moon, and the stars.
sentences.zh: 许多免费的自由和开源操作系统都非常积极地宣传自己，承诺太阳、月亮和星星。

sentences.en: Whereas the truth for many is that they’re assembled by a small team with a shoestring budget (or none at all) from years-old software offered by grumpy developers they don’t have a great relationship with.
sentences.zh: 然而对许多人来说，事实是它们是由一个小团队组装而成的，预算很少（或者根本没有），而这些软件是由脾气暴躁的开发人员提供的，与他们没有很好的关系。

blocks.html_en: Users then don’t understand the full picture of who’s responsible for what, or the level of support they should expect and from whom.

sentences.en: Users then don’t understand the full picture of who’s responsible for what, or the level of support they should expect and from whom.
sentences.zh: 这样，用户就无法全面了解谁负责什么，或者他们应该期望从谁那里得到多少支持。

blocks.html_en: This is the reason why KDE Linux includes tons of qualifiers and provisos in its marketing material. We don’t want to over-promise! It’s a small project built by a small team. I think every piece of free software should be clear about who should use it… and who shouldn’t. Don’t over-promise! It’s a recipe for frustration and disappointment on all sides.

sentences.en: This is the reason why KDE Linux includes tons of qualifiers and provisos in its marketing material.
sentences.zh: 这就是 KDE Linux 在其营销材料中包含大量限定词和限制条件的原因。

sentences.en: We don’t want to over-promise!
sentences.zh: 我们不想过度承诺！

sentences.en: It’s a small project built by a small team.
sentences.zh: 这是一个由小团队构建的小项目。

sentences.en: I think every piece of free software should be clear about who should use it…
sentences.zh: 我认为每一个自由软件都应该清楚谁应该使用它......

sentences.en: and who shouldn’t.
sentences.zh: 谁不应该。

sentences.en: Don’t over-promise!
sentences.zh: 不要过度承诺！

sentences.en: It’s a recipe for frustration and disappointment on all sides.
sentences.zh: 这会让各方都感到沮丧和失望。

blocks.html_en: And if you want an OS with professional backing, you’ll likely need to pay for it.

sentences.en: And if you want an OS with professional backing, you’ll likely need to pay for it.
sentences.zh: 如果您想要一个有专业支持的操作系统，您可能需要付费。

blocks.html_en: So what’s the solution?

sentences.en: So what’s the solution?
sentences.zh: 那么解决办法是什么呢？

blocks.html_en: As a distributor, I think you need to sort out what kind of relationship you want to have with the developers of the upstream software you ship.

sentences.en: As a distributor, I think you need to sort out what kind of relationship you want to have with the developers of the upstream software you ship.
sentences.zh: 作为分销商，我认为您需要弄清楚您希望与您发布的上游软件的开发人员建立什么样的关系。

blocks.html_en: Do you want to be able to have a distant relationship? Then I recommend abandoning discrete releases and adopting the rolling release approach.

sentences.en: Do you want to be able to have a distant relationship?
sentences.zh: 你想拥有远距离的关系吗？

sentences.en: Then I recommend abandoning discrete releases and adopting the rolling release approach.
sentences.zh: 那么我建议放弃离散发布，采用滚动发布的方式。

blocks.html_en: In this model, you take upstream software the moment it’s released — or at least soon afterwards. Then the conflict disappears! Almost all bugs become upstream bugs, and you can direct users to upstream devs without getting any push-back from them. Everything remaining is an issue that you can fix in your OS.

sentences.en: In this model, you take upstream software the moment it’s released — or at least soon afterwards.
sentences.zh: 在这种模型中，您可以在软件发布后立即获取上游软件 - 或者至少在发布后不久。

sentences.en: Then the conflict disappears!
sentences.zh: 那么冲突就消失了！

sentences.en: Almost all bugs become upstream bugs, and you can direct users to upstream devs without getting any push-back from them.
sentences.zh: 几乎所有错误都会成为上游错误，您可以将用户引导至上游开发人员，而不会受到他们的任何拒绝。

sentences.en: Everything remaining is an issue that you can fix in your OS.
sentences.zh: 剩下的所有问题都是您可以在操作系统中修复的问题。

blocks.html_en: Rolling release OSs can still have good relationships with their upstreams, of course. But it isn’t as critical.

sentences.en: Rolling release OSs can still have good relationships with their upstreams, of course.
sentences.zh: 当然，滚动发布操作系统仍然可以与其上游保持良好的关系。

sentences.en: But it isn’t as critical.
sentences.zh: 但这并不那么重要。

blocks.html_en: Don’t want to be a rolling release? That’s fine. Then you need to work closely with your upstreams.

sentences.en: Don’t want to be a rolling release?
sentences.zh: 不想滚动发布？

sentences.en: That’s fine.
sentences.zh: 没关系。

sentences.en: Then you need to work closely with your upstreams.
sentences.zh: 然后你需要与你的上游密切合作。

blocks.html_en: Communicate your users’ complaints and bugs upstream, help drive fixes, and then ship them. Many software developers already release bug-fix versions in addition to feature versions. If they do… ship them! Don’t ignore them; this will make your upstreams grumpier.

sentences.en: Communicate your users’ complaints and bugs upstream, help drive fixes, and then ship them.
sentences.zh: 向上游传达用户的投诉和错误，帮助推动修复，然后交付它们。

sentences.en: Many software developers already release bug-fix versions in addition to feature versions.
sentences.zh: 除了功能版本之外，许多软件开发人员已经发布了错误修复版本。

sentences.en: If they do…
sentences.zh: 如果他们这样做……

sentences.en: ship them!
sentences.zh: 运送它们！

sentences.en: Don’t ignore them; this will make your upstreams grumpier.
sentences.zh: 不要忽视他们；这会让你的上游变得更加脾气暴躁。

blocks.html_en: If any of your upstreams release a “long-term support” version of their software, ship that. If they don’t, you can even work with them to create one if they’re amenable to the idea, and help to support it.

sentences.en: If any of your upstreams release a “long-term support” version of their software, ship that.
sentences.zh: 如果您的任何上游发布了其软件的“长期支持”版本，请发布该版本。

sentences.en: If they don’t, you can even work with them to create one if they’re amenable to the idea, and help to support it.
sentences.zh: 如果他们不这样做，如果他们愿意接受这个想法，你甚至可以与他们一起创建一个，并帮助支持它。

blocks.html_en: This is more work, obviously. So if your team is small, it may not be feasible to do for every piece of software you ship. But you can try for the most important ones: the Linux kernel, systemd, Mesa, Libinput, PipeWire, NetworkManager, BlueZ, udisks, CUPS, and KDE or GNOME.

sentences.en: This is more work, obviously.
sentences.zh: 显然，这是更多的工作。

sentences.en: So if your team is small, it may not be feasible to do for every piece of software you ship.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果您的团队规模较小，则可能无法对您发布的每一个软件都进行操作。

sentences.en: But you can try for the most important ones: the Linux kernel, systemd, Mesa, Libinput, PipeWire, NetworkManager, BlueZ, udisks, CUPS, and KDE or GNOME.
sentences.zh: 但您可以尝试最重要的：Linux 内核、systemd、Mesa、Libinput、PipeWire、NetworkManager、BlueZ、udi​​sks、CUPS 以及 KDE 或 GNOME。

blocks.html_en: But that’s the important point: shipping a high-quality discrete release OS is more work than producing a competent rolling release. No way around it.

sentences.en: But that’s the important point: shipping a high-quality discrete release OS is more work than producing a competent rolling release.
sentences.zh: 但这是重要的一点：交付高质量的离散版本操作系统比制作合格的滚动版本需要更多的工作。

sentences.en: No way around it.
sentences.zh: 没有办法解决它。

blocks.html_en: So a way to make this approach more feasible is to reduce your OS’s scope of concern.

sentences.en: So a way to make this approach more feasible is to reduce your OS’s scope of concern.
sentences.zh: 因此，使这种方法更加可行的一种方法是减少操作系统的关注范围。

blocks.html_en: For example, you can delegate app distribution to a third party like Flathub, the Snap store, AppImageHub, etc. This lets you focus on just the base OS and its desktop environment, while apps are rolling, which means they’ll accept bug reports about them. Personally, I think there’s a lot to like about this model.

sentences.en: For example, you can delegate app distribution to a third party like Flathub, the Snap store, AppImageHub, etc. This lets you focus on just the base OS and its desktop environment, while apps are rolling, which means they’ll accept bug reports about them.
sentences.zh: 例如，您可以将应用程序分发委托给第三方，例如 Flathub、Snap 商店、AppImageHub 等。这使您可以在应用程序滚动时仅关注基本操作系统及其桌面环境，这意味着它们将接受有关它们的错误报告。

sentences.en: Personally, I think there’s a lot to like about this model.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我认为这个模型有很多值得喜欢的地方。

blocks.html_en: It also helps if you don’t let the software get too out of date. Up to 6 months old? Your upstreams are likely fine with this, and will accept bug reports. 12 months? Mostly fine. 2 years? That starts to get painful for your upstream developers if users are still reporting bugs to them. 3 year or more? Very painful. We pretty much have to direct them to their OS vendor.

sentences.en: It also helps if you don’t let the software get too out of date.
sentences.zh: 如果您不让软件变得太过时，它也会有所帮助。

sentences.en: Up to 6 months old?
sentences.zh: 最多 6 个月大？

sentences.en: Your upstreams are likely fine with this, and will accept bug reports.
sentences.zh: 您的上游可能对此表示满意，并且会接受错误报告。

sentences.en: 12 months?
sentences.zh: 12个月？

sentences.en: Mostly fine.
sentences.zh: 大部分都还好。

sentences.en: 2 years?
That starts to get painful for your upstream developers if users are still reporting bugs to them.
sentences.zh: 如果用户仍然向上游开发人员报告错误，这就会开始让他们感到痛苦。

sentences.en: 3 year or more?
sentences.zh: 3年或以上？

sentences.en: Very painful.
sentences.zh: 非常痛苦。

sentences.en: We pretty much have to direct them to their OS vendor.
sentences.zh: 我们几乎必须将他们引导至他们的操作系统供应商。

blocks.html_en: What’s not the solution?

sentences.en: What’s not the solution?
sentences.zh: 没有解决办法是什么？

blocks.html_en: Package 2+ year-old frozen releases of as much upstream software as you can, and then ignore the upstreams, their bug-fix releases, and their complaints about this.

sentences.en: Package 2+ year-old frozen releases of as much upstream software as you can, and then ignore the upstreams, their bug-fix releases, and their complaints about this.
sentences.zh: 尽可能多地打包 2 年以上的上游软件的冻结版本，然后忽略上游软件、他们的错误修复版本以及他们对此的抱怨。

blocks.html_en: This is the worst of all worlds!

sentences.en: This is the worst of all worlds!
sentences.zh: 这是世界上最糟糕的事情！

blocks.html_en: • Users get outdated software that’s full of bugs and security holes fixed long ago

sentences.en: Users get outdated software that’s full of bugs and security holes fixed long ago
sentences.zh: 用户得到的是过时的软件，其中充满了很久以前就修复的错误和安全漏洞

blocks.html_en: • Software developers get un-actionable bug reports from angry and confused users

sentences.en: Software developers get un-actionable bug reports from angry and confused users
sentences.zh: 软件开发人员从愤怒和困惑的用户那里得到无法操作的错误报告

blocks.html_en: • Distributors get frustrated communications from software developers that damage or destroy important relationships

sentences.en: Distributors get frustrated communications from software developers that damage or destroy important relationships
sentences.zh: 分销商与软件开发人员的沟通受挫，从而损害或破坏了重要的关系

blocks.html_en: Please don’t do this. It isn’t sustainable over time, and will cause a leak of the most desirable users who make an active choice to use your OS.

sentences.en: Please don’t do this.
sentences.zh: 请不要这样做。

sentences.en: It isn’t sustainable over time, and will cause a leak of the most desirable users who make an active choice to use your OS.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，这种情况是不可持续的，并且会导致主动选择使用您的操作系统的最理想用户的流失。

blocks.html_en: How do I know this?

sentences.en: How do I know this?
sentences.zh: 我怎么知道这个？

blocks.html_en: Well, I don’t, but I can make an educated guess based on some of the few statistics we do have. A big one is the OS market share on ProtonDB as compiled by BoilingSteam, which counts Linux gamers:

sentences.en: Well, I don’t, but I can make an educated guess based on some of the few statistics we do have.
sentences.zh: 好吧，我不知道，但我可以根据我们现有的一些统计数据做出有根据的猜测。

sentences.en: A big one is the OS market share on ProtonDB as compiled by BoilingSteam, which counts Linux gamers:
sentences.zh: 其中一个重要因素是由 BoilingSteam 编制的 ProtonDB 操作系统市场份额，其中统计了 Linux 游戏玩家：

blocks.html_en: In 2019, the “rolling and rolling-ish (e.g. Fedora)” OSs made up a little over 36% of the total users.

sentences.en: In 2019, the “rolling and rolling-ish (e.g. Fedora)” OSs made up a little over 36% of the total users.
sentences.zh: 2019 年，“滚动式和滚动式（例如 Fedora）”操作系统占总用户的比例略高于 36%。

blocks.html_en: In 2026, they’re up to 71%.

sentences.en: In 2026, they’re up to 71%.
sentences.zh: 到 2026 年，这一比例将达到 71%。

blocks.html_en: This isn’t everyone, of course; only gamers using ProtonDB. But still, it’s quite a change. Clearly gamers believe rolling-release OSs cater to their needs better than discrete-release OSs do.

sentences.en: This isn’t everyone, of course; only gamers using ProtonDB.
sentences.zh: 当然，这并不是所有人。仅使用 ProtonDB 的游戏玩家。

sentences.en: But still, it’s quite a change.
sentences.zh: 但这仍然是一个很大的变化。

sentences.en: Clearly gamers believe rolling-release OSs cater to their needs better than discrete-release OSs do.
sentences.zh: 显然，游戏玩家相信滚动发布的操作系统比离散发布的操作系统更能满足他们的需求。

blocks.html_en: So, if you ship a discrete release OS, and you don’t want to or can’t switch it to a rolling or semi-rolling release cadence, please please please work with your upstreams! As a (sadly now only merely occasional) upstream KDE software dev, I can tell you my favorite distributors to work with are the ones who engage with KDE and help get problems solved. It’s a lot of fun when all parties focus on problem-solving.

sentences.en: So, if you ship a discrete release OS, and you don’t want to or can’t switch it to a rolling or semi-rolling release cadence, please please please work with your upstreams!
sentences.zh: 因此，如果您发布了离散版本操作系统，并且您不想或无法将其切换为滚动或半滚动发布节奏，请与您的上游合作！

sentences.en: As a (sadly now only merely occasional) upstream KDE software dev, I can tell you my favorite distributors to work with are the ones who engage with KDE and help get problems solved.
sentences.zh: 作为一名（遗憾的是现在只是偶尔的）上游 KDE 软件开发人员，我可以告诉你我最喜欢合作的分销商是那些参与 KDE 并帮助解决问题的分销商。

sentences.en: It’s a lot of fun when all parties focus on problem-solving.
sentences.zh: 当各方都专注于解决问题时，这会很有趣。

content_html_zh:
考虑这个假设：

操作系统 (OS) 附带软件的 1.5 版本。

同时，该软件的最新版本是3.0。

该操作系统的用户在版本 1.5 中遇到问题，或者有新功能的想法。 他们应该联系谁？

由于 Ubuntu、Debian、openSUSE Leap 和 Linux Mint 等离散版本操作系统的存在，这一假设变得具体。 它们故意将其发布的软件的某些版本冻结一段时间——即使它们的上游已经发布了较新的版本。

它现在成为新闻焦点是因为最近 Linux Mint 发生了一场混乱； GNOME Calendar 的开发者要求 Linux Mint 修补支持链接并更改品牌，随后在该问题出于可以理解的原因被锁定后发表了一篇相当煽动性的博客文章。

这让我感到难过，因为沟通不畅是可以避免的，现在围绕这个话题的讨论很大一部分是关于语气而不是话题本身——这是不关心语气的可预见的结果。 但我离题了。

我认为这是一个重要的话题，所以我想分享一下我对这种情况的看法。

软件开发商有责任吗？

软件开发人员编写了软件。 软件坏了。 故事结束。

生活要是这么简单就好了！

收到了数以千计的关于离散版本操作系统中 KDE 软件旧版本的无法采取行动的错误报告，这些错误报告在几个月或几年前就已修复（但操作系统发行商并未向后移植！）... 我可以告诉你这非常令人沮丧。

但我们在自由开源软件 (FOSS) 世界中通过自由软件许可证提供我们的软件；我们需要为操作系统以我们没有预料到或不感兴趣的方式分发我们的软件以及用户的错误报告做好准备。 这就是生活。

我们在 KDE 中的解决方案是一个机器人，它可以自动关闭不受支持的 Plasma 版本的错误报告，并且我们最终可能会扩展系统以涵盖我们的应用程序和框架。

它并不完美，但基本上可以解决问题。 我之所以能这么说，是因为我最近看到的大多数新 Plasma 错误报告都来自 Arch、OpenSUSE Tumbleweed 和 Fedora KDE（不是真正的滚动版本，但已经足够接近）等滚动发布操作系统的用户，而且我认为大多数都是可操作的。

对于离散版本操作系统的用户来说，这有点糟糕，他们会得到一个机器人告诉他们，他们刚刚为报告错误所做的工作是无用的。 但他们也受益于滚动发布的用户，有效地成为他们的免费质量保证（QA）。 权衡。

经销商有责任吗？

如果我的车坏了，我会责怪汽车公司，而不是卖给他们坏零件的供应商。

（好吧，如果我是一个汽车迷，也许我也会责怪他们，但这是例外！）

最终，操作系统分销商是组装最终产品的汽车公司。 他们的工作是进行充分的质量检查并与供应商（上游软件开发人员）合作，以确保最终产品闪闪发光。

当然，一辆新车要花费好几个月的工资，并包括多年保修，而大多数自由和开源操作系统则没有，而且是免费分发的。 因此，期望需要稍微缓和一些。

我认为这就是沟通中断的地方。 许多免费的自由和开源操作系统都非常积极地宣传自己，承诺太阳、月亮和星星。 然而对许多人来说，事实是它们是由一个小团队组装而成的，预算很少（或者根本没有），而这些软件是由脾气暴躁的开发人员提供的，与他们没有很好的关系。

这样，用户就无法全面了解谁负责什么，或者他们应该期望从谁那里得到多少支持。

这就是 KDE Linux 在其营销材料中包含大量限定词和限制条件的原因。 我们不想过度承诺！ 这是一个由小团队构建的小项目。 我认为每一个自由软件都应该清楚谁应该使用它...... 谁不应该。 不要过度承诺！ 这会让各方都感到沮丧和失望。

如果您想要一个有专业支持的操作系统，您可能需要付费。

那么解决办法是什么呢？

作为分销商，我认为您需要弄清楚您希望与您发布的上游软件的开发人员建立什么样的关系。

你想拥有远距离的关系吗？ 那么我建议放弃离散发布，采用滚动发布的方式。

在这种模型中，您可以在软件发布后立即获取上游软件 - 或者至少在发布后不久。 那么冲突就消失了！ 几乎所有错误都会成为上游错误，您可以将用户引导至上游开发人员，而不会受到他们的任何拒绝。 剩下的所有问题都是您可以在操作系统中修复的问题。

当然，滚动发布操作系统仍然可以与其上游保持良好的关系。 但这并不那么重要。

不想滚动发布？ 没关系。 然后你需要与你的上游密切合作。

向上游传达用户的投诉和错误，帮助推动修复，然后交付它们。 除了功能版本之外，许多软件开发人员已经发布了错误修复版本。 如果他们这样做…… 运送它们！ 不要忽视他们；这会让你的上游变得更加脾气暴躁。

如果您的任何上游发布了其软件的“长期支持”版本，请发布该版本。 如果他们不这样做，如果他们愿意接受这个想法，你甚至可以与他们一起创建一个，并帮助支持它。

显然，这是更多的工作。 因此，如果您的团队规模较小，则可能无法对您发布的每一个软件都进行操作。 但您可以尝试最重要的：Linux 内核、systemd、Mesa、Libinput、PipeWire、NetworkManager、BlueZ、udi​​sks、CUPS 以及 KDE 或 GNOME。

但这是重要的一点：交付高质量的离散版本操作系统比制作合格的滚动版本需要更多的工作。 没有办法解决它。

因此，使这种方法更加可行的一种方法是减少操作系统的关注范围。

例如，您可以将应用程序分发委托给第三方，例如 Flathub、Snap 商店、AppImageHub 等。这使您可以在应用程序滚动时仅关注基本操作系统及其桌面环境，这意味着它们将接受有关它们的错误报告。 就我个人而言，我认为这个模型有很多值得喜欢的地方。

如果您不让软件变得太过时，它也会有所帮助。 最多 6 个月大？ 您的上游可能对此表示满意，并且会接受错误报告。 12个月？ 大部分都还好。 2年？ 如果用户仍然向上游开发人员报告错误，这就会开始让他们感到痛苦。 3年或以上？ 非常痛苦。 我们几乎必须将他们引导至他们的操作系统供应商。

没有解决办法是什么？

尽可能多地打包 2 年以上的上游软件的冻结版本，然后忽略上游软件、他们的错误修复版本以及他们对此的抱怨。

这是世界上最糟糕的事情！

  • 用户得到的是过时的软件，其中充满了很久以前就修复的错误和安全漏洞
  • 软件开发人员从愤怒和困惑的用户那里得到无法操作的错误报告
  • 分销商与软件开发人员的沟通受挫，从而损害或破坏了重要的关系

请不要这样做。 随着时间的推移，这种情况是不可持续的，并且会导致主动选择使用您的操作系统的最理想用户的流失。

我怎么知道这个？

好吧，我不知道，但我可以根据我们现有的一些统计数据做出有根据的猜测。 其中一个重要因素是由 BoilingSteam 编制的 ProtonDB 操作系统市场份额，其中统计了 Linux 游戏玩家：

2019 年，“滚动式和滚动式（例如 Fedora）”操作系统占总用户的比例略高于 36%。

到 2026 年，这一比例将达到 71%。

当然，这并不是所有人。仅使用 ProtonDB 的游戏玩家。 但这仍然是一个很大的变化。 显然，游戏玩家相信滚动发布的操作系统比离散发布的操作系统更能满足他们的需求。

因此，如果您发布了离散版本操作系统，并且您不想或无法将其切换为滚动或半滚动发布节奏，请与您的上游合作！ 作为一名（遗憾的是现在只是偶尔的）上游 KDE 软件开发人员，我可以告诉你我最喜欢合作的分销商是那些参与 KDE 并帮助解决问题的分销商。 当各方都专注于解决问题时，这会很有趣。
